item.title: How to Revive the Art of Hanging Out
item.description: Modern life makes it harder to seek out places to just be, but it’s not impossible.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Fattal

item.title: A Beguiling Film About the Downsides of Pop Stardom
item.description: Mother Maryoffers a spooky spin on what it takes to stay famous.
item.dc:creator: David Sims

item.title: Is Hurry the Great Enemy of Spiritual Life?
item.description: Pastor John Mark Comer has won a massive audience by encouraging his followers to free themselves from the gnawing sense that there is always more to do.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Walecki

item.title: The Film That Explains Contemporary America
item.description: The Sorrow and the Pityhas lessons for how authoritarianism takes root—and how to fight against it.
item.dc:creator: David A. Graham

item.title: Ukraine Has Finally Given Up on Trump
item.description: Zelensky has written off the United States.
item.dc:creator: Phillips Payson O’Brien

item.title: The FBI Director Is MIA
item.description: Kash Patel has alarmed colleagues with episodes of excessive drinking and unexplained absences.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fitzpatrick

item.title: The Donald J. Trump Guide to Classic Fairy Tales
item.description: Has the president failed to learn the lessons of classic cautionary fables—or does he just understand them in his own novel ways?
item.dc:creator: David A. Graham

item.title: Today’sAtlanticTrivia: First Ladies
item.description: Test your knowledge—and read our stories for a little extra help.
item.dc:creator: Drew Goins

item.title: Iran Had a Doomsday Weapon All Along
item.description: Control of a vital waterway gives Tehran the deterrence power it’s always wanted.
item.dc:creator: Alan Eyre

item.title: One of These Trump Threats Is Not Like the Others
item.description: Trump has developed a reputation for backing down from his most over-the-top threats, but dismissing his words is a mistake.
item.dc:creator: Marie-Rose Sheinerman

item.title: Breaking Free From Alex Jones
item.description: A former Infowars employee on radicalization, lies, and getting out
item.dc:creator: Charlie Warzel

item.title: The First Draft of Cultural History
item.description: Lena Dunham’s new memoir is a fascinating primary source of Hollywood in the 2010s.
item.dc:creator: Boris Kachka

item.title: The Real Crisis ofThe Pitt
item.description: What the hit show’s approach to Dr. Robby reveals
item.dc:creator: David Sims

item.title: Photos of the Week: Glacier Performance, Gorilla Birthday, Moon Return
item.description: Cherry blossoms in bloom in Japan, preparations for a humanoid-robot half marathon in China, a boisterous water festival in Thailand, a scene from Coachella in California, and much more
item.dc:creator: Alan Taylor

item.title: The Scapegoat Scam
item.description: Hungarians stopped falling for an authoritarian’s trick.
item.dc:creator: Adam Serwer

item.title: The Allbirds Pivot Is a Terrible Idea … Right?
item.description: Its turn to AI could be an escape hatch for a company with nothing to lose.
item.dc:creator: Will Gottsegen

item.title: RFK Jr.’s New Normal
item.description: As Trump nominates a new, uncontroversial CDC director, a more restrained version of the health secretary is appearing.
item.dc:creator: Tom Bartlett

item.title: Israel Moderates Are Losing the Democratic Party
item.description: Their position has become untenable. But liberal Zionists can adapt.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Chait

item.title: Today’sAtlanticTrivia: Name the Pol, Then the Actor
item.description: Test your knowledge—and read our stories for a little extra help.
item.dc:creator: Drew Goins

item.title: The Tyranny of AI Everywhere
item.description: Sneakers? Why stop there?
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Petri
